subject: [PATCH v3 2/3] fuse: use release/acquire for fch->initialized

2026-07-16.patch_summary: This patch addresses a potential issue in the FUSE (Filesystem in Userspace) code where lockless readers may access the 'initialized' flag of a connection before it has been properly set by the writer, leading to undefined behavior. To fix this, the patch introduces release/acquire semantics for writing and reading the 'initialized' flag using smp_store_release() and smp_load_acquire(). This ensures that writers and readers are properly synchronized, preventing potential issues. The changes are made in three files: cuse.c, dev.c, and dev_uring.c. The patch is not marked as stable because it addresses a rare edge case that should not occur on well-behaved servers.
